perturbation.original_text: 8. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS PROVIDED TO RECIPIENT “AS- IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, AS TO ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE. Both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable federal, state and local laws, rules, regulations, statutes, ordinances , codes,
perturbation.changed_text: 8. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UNDER THIS AGREEMENT IS PROVIDED TO RECIPIENT “AS- IS” WITHOUT ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, AS TO ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE. Both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable federal laws, rules, regulations, statutes, ordinances , codes,
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable state and local laws, the contract creates a potential legal contradiction, especially concerning Iowa's specific regulations on data security and privacy, which are applicable to confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 554G.3 mandates reasonable security measures for personal information. By removing the warranty of compliance with state laws, the contract weakens the assurance that such measures will be followed, potentially violating Iowa law if confidential information includes personal data.

perturbation.justification: The original contract warranted compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local laws. The changed contract removes the warranty of compliance with state and local laws. The Law Explanation states that Iowa Code § 554G.3 mandates reasonable security measures for personal information. By removing the warranty of compliance with state laws, the contract weakens the assurance that such measures will be followed, potentially violating Iowa law if confidential information includes personal data. Scraped Law Snippet 1 is a PDF that does not contain the Iowa Code § 554G.3 section and is therefore not useful. The Perturbation Explanation states that removing the clause that both parties represent and warrant they will comply with all applicable state and local laws, the contract creates a potential legal contradiction, especially concerning Iowa's specific regulations on data security and privacy, which are applicable to confidential information. This omission creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. LAW AND VENUE. The laws of the State of Iowa , without giving effect to its conflicts of law principles, govern all matters arising out of or relating to t his Agreement. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and forum and/or venue in, any federal or state court of competent jurisdiction located in Iowa for the purposes of adjudicating any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Litigation or legal proceedings which arise out of or relate to this Agreement are to be conducted before a judge and not a jury.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. LAW AND VENUE. The laws of the State of Iowa , without giving effect to its conflicts of law principles, govern all matters arising out of or relating to t his Agreement. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and forum and/or venue in, any federal or state court of competent jurisdiction located in Iowa for the purposes of adjudicating any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Litigation or legal proceedings which arise out of or relate to this Agreement may be conducted before a judge or jury at the company's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Iowa law grants parties the right to a jury trial in civil cases. Removing the 'and not a jury' clause and stating that it is 'at the company's discretion' directly contradicts this right, making the contract potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article I, Section 9 of the Iowa Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. This modification, giving the company discretion over jury trials, directly contradicts this constitutional right.

Article I, Iowa Constitution From Ballotpedia Jump to: navigation , search Iowa Constitution Preamble Articles I • II • III • IV • V • VI • VII • VIII • IX • X • XI • XII Contents 1 Section 1 1.1 Amendments 2 Section 1A 2.1 Amendments 3 Section 2 4 Section 3 5 Section 4 6 Section 5 7 Section 6 8 Section 7 9 Section 8 10 Section 9 11 Section 10 12 Section 11 12.1 Amendments 13 Section 12 14 Section 13 15 Section 14 16 Section 15 17 Section 16 18 Section 17 19 Section 18 19.1 Amendments 20 Section 19 21 Section 20 22 Section 21 23 Section 22 24 Section 23 25 Section 24 26 Section 25 27 See also 28 External links 29 Additional reading 30 Footnotes Article I of the Iowa Constitution is labeled Bill of Rights. It has 25 sections. Section 1 Text of Section 1: Rights of Persons All men and women are, by nature, free and equal, and have certain inalienable rights--among which are those of enjoying and defending life and liberty, acquiring, possessing and protecting property, and pursuing and obtaining safety and happiness. [1] Amendments Amended in 1998 with the approval of Iowa Equal Rights Act, Chapter 216 (1998) . Section 1A Text of Section 1A: Right to keep and bear arms The right of the people to keep and bear arms shall not be infringed. The sovereign state of Iowa affirms and recognizes this right to be a fundamental individual right. Any and all restrictions of this right shall be subject to strict scrutiny. Amendments Section 1A was added with the approval of Amendment 1 on November 8, 2022. Section 2 Text of Section 2: Political Power All political power is inherent in the people. Government is instituted for the protection, security, and benefit of the people, and they have the right, at all times, to alter or reform the same, whenever the public good may require it. [1] Section 3 Text of Section 3: Religion The general assembly shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; nor shall any person be compelled to attend any place of worship, pay tithes, taxes, or other rates for building or repairing places of worship, or the maintenance of any minister, or ministry. [1] Section 4 Text of Section 4: Religious Test--Witnesses No religious test shall be required as a qualification for any office, or public trust, an
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the right to a jury trial as seemingly guaranteed by the Iowa Constitution. The original text explicitly denies a jury trial, while the changed text grants the company discretion over whether a jury trial is allowed. This undermines the right to a jury trial for the other party, as supported by the Law Explanation and the Perturbation Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 references Article I of the Iowa Constitution, which includes the 'Bill of Rights,' suggesting fundamental rights are at stake. The change from 'not a jury' to 'company's discretion' removes a key legal protection, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall take measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This section dictates how Confidential Information should be protected. By removing the 'no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information', the contract fails to meet data security standards outlined in Iowa law, opening loopholes for insufficient protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 554G.3 requires entities to implement and maintain reasonable security measures to protect personal information. By removing the standard of protecting confidential information with at least the same measures used for their own information, the contract offers a weak standard of protection, potentially violating Iowa law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text removes a critical component of the original text, namely that the recipient must protect Confidential Information with no less rigor than they protect their own. The Law Explanation states that Iowa Code § 554G.3 requires entities to implement and maintain reasonable security measures to protect personal information, and the modified text provides a weaker standard of protection. Scraped Law Snippet 1, while partially unreadable, appears to pertain to data security measures, supporting the interpretation that the original text offered a stronger safeguard in alignment with data protection standards, whereas the changed text omits a concrete baseline, thereby potentially violating Iowa law.

perturbation.original_text: All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: This section focuses on the return of materials. By omitting the clause about 'promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement', the contract contradicts Iowa's data disposal laws, which require the proper disposal of personal information to prevent unauthorized access.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 554G.3 mandates the implementation of reasonable measures for destroying or arranging for the destruction of customer's personal information. By removing the clause regarding the return or destruction of confidential information upon request or termination, the contract fails to assure that data will be properly disposed of, potentially violating Iowa law.

perturbation.justification: The modified text omits the requirement for the recipient to return or destroy confidential information upon request or termination of the agreement. The Law Explanation states that Iowa Code § 554G.3 mandates reasonable measures for destroying customer's personal information, and the snippet mentions implementation for destruction of customer's personal information. By removing the clause regarding the return or destruction, the modified contract fails to assure that data will be properly disposed of, potentially violating Iowa law.
